detailedExemplarData.title: Finding the volume of my Kiddush cup

subject.title: Mathematics Analysis and Approaches (AA)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics Analysis and Approaches** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **number and algebra, functions, geometry and trigonometry, statistics and probability, and calculus**, with a strong focus on **theoretical understanding, formal mathematical reasoning, and analytical problem-solving**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Non-calculator written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 2** | Calculator allowed written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 3** | Calculator allowed problem-solving | N/A | 60 minutes | 20% (HL only) |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal Assessment | 20 hours | 20 hours | 20% |

strengths: Clear, coherent narrative structure with a logical progression from context to conclusion
Wellâorganized sections with introduction, subheadings, and referenced figures
Comprehensive worksâcited list reflecting a wide range of relevant sources
weaknesses: Minor breaks in flow (typographical slips, stray characters) interrupt coherence
Some unnecessary bulk (e.g., full polynomial expansion) reduces conciseness
Inconsistent citation formatting and figure numbering
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand assessments place coherence and organization in band 3, while conciseness sits at band 2. On balance, the work demonstrates a clear, logical development and solid structure (band 3) but includes excess detail and minor presentation errors, justifying a score of 3.

strengths: Mostly consistent and accurate use of mathematical notation and symbols
Effective integration of multiple representations (tables, graphs, diagrams)
Good logical structure with clear explanations linking each step to modelling goals
weaknesses: Occasional notation slips (missing minus signs, inconsistent variable names)
Units and significantâfigure rationale are sometimes omitted
Typos in integral bounds and labels reduce clarity
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All three strandsâlanguage and notation, representations, and logical clarityâare assessed at band 3. While the communication is coherent and effective, minor errors and inconsistencies prevent a band 4, making a score of 3 appropriate.

strengths: Investigation driven by a personal context and choice of artefact
Clear narrative linking family tradition to mathematical objectives
Good initiative shown in realâworld data collection and cost analysis extension
weaknesses: Mathematical approach follows standard syllabus methods rather than an innovative route
Independent thinking is solid but not outstanding
Creative elements are present but not sustained at an exceptional level
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Each personal engagement strand falls in band 2: evidence of independent thinking, a clear personal approach, and good creativity are all present but not exceptional. Thus a score of 2 reflects the studentâs solid but not outstanding personal engagement.

strengths: Meaningful reflection on measurement accuracy and validation of volume
Thoughtful analysis comparing original and optimised designs, with cost evaluation
Clear evaluation of outcomes with suggestions for future extensions
weaknesses: Reflection remains largely descriptive rather than deeply critical
Limited quantitative sensitivity or environmental analysis
Key assumptions (e.g., wall thickness) are noted but not thoroughly scrutinized
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All reflection strands are at band 2: there is meaningful reflection, thoughtful analysis, and clear evaluation. The work does not yet exhibit the depth or critical insight required for band 3, so a score of 2 is justified.

strengths: Comprehensive application of SL mathematics: regression, integration, differentiation, optimisation
Correct unit conversions and use of technology for integrals and regression
Logical derivations of volume and surface-area formulae with appropriate calculus tests
weaknesses: Minor algebraic and rounding errors (e.g., missing minus signs, inconsistent bounds)
Reliance on CAS outputs without full verification of intermediate steps
Increment choice (adding 1 cm) lacks mathematical justification
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Two strands are at band 5 (relevance and level appropriateness) and one at band 4 (understanding with minor errors). The overall use of mathematics demonstrates comprehensive, context-integrated SL techniques despite small slips, warranting a score of 5.

strandSelections.reasoning: Explanations accompany every major calculation, linking each algebraic step to the modelling goal, and justifying decisions such as the choice of a cubic regression.  Occasional formatting issues (run-on equations, inconsistent variable names) limit absolute clarity, but the overall logical structure is good and easy to follow.

Derivatives, integrals and surface-area calculations are mostly accurate; the numerical volume is sensible and cross-checked.  Nonetheless, there are minor algebraic slips (missing minus sign in expanded polynomial, rounding inconsistencies) and the surface-area integral is accepted from CAS without verification.  These small errors lower the strand to âgood understanding and mostly correctâ (band 4).

The student employs tables of data, labelled diagrams of the cup, regression graphs, geometric sketches and algebraic expressions.  These representations complement one another and are integrated into the argument, meeting the descriptor for effective (band 3) use.  To reach the highest band the work would need still wider or more sophisticated representation (e.g. dynamic technology output or error bars) and flawless labelling.

The investigation follows a clear narrative: personal context â aim â modelling â calculation â optimisation â cost analysis â conclusion.  Each section builds on the previous one so the reader can follow the logical thread without getting lost.  Minor breaks in flow (e.g. typographical slips, duplicated symbols) occasionally interrupt the argument, so the development is good rather than exemplary.

Gathering physical data, applying calculus to design, and extending the study to a cost analysis demonstrate creativity and initiative beyond a single routine problem.  Nonetheless, the optimisation itself follows a typical derivative-equals-zero route and so is âgoodâ rather than âexceptionalâ.

Results are analysed to compare original and optimised surface areas and to draw cost conclusions.  The discussion recognises some limitations (price volatility, manufacturing costs) but stops short of deep, quantitative sensitivity analysis, which would be needed for the top band.

The student evaluates the outcome by weighing material costs and acknowledging that real-world factors could change decisions.  There is a clear statement of what was achieved and how the model might be extended, satisfying the âclear evaluationâ standard without reaching the comprehensive, forward-looking depth of band 3.

The student comments on measurement accuracy, validates the volume with water displacement and notes assumptions about constant thickness.  These elements constitute meaningful reflection, though they are brief and largely descriptive rather than probing, so they align with band 2.

Appropriate symbols (Ï, â, derivative primes) and function notation are used throughout, and variables are defined when first introduced.  A few slips (missing minus signs, mismatched brackets) and occasional plain-text code fragments prevent the language from being fully consistent, hence âmostlyâ rather than âcompletelyâ accurate.

The narrative is strongly personal: family tradition, grandmotherâs shop, and the studentâs own aesthetic judgements drive the investigation.  This clear personal approach is evident but not highly innovative in its mathematical execution, placing it comfortably in band 2.

Most of the discussion is pertinent, but several long verbatim formulae, full calculator screens and repeated numerical values add bulk without advancing the argument.  The exploration could be trimmed significantly (e.g. omitting the full polynomial expansion) while retaining all mathematical content, so it is only âmostlyâ relevant rather than fully concise.

Calculus (integration, differentiation), regression modelling, optimisation, surface-of-revolution formulae and densityâmass conversions are all squarely at SL level and are applied to multiple aspects of the problem.  The mathematics is integrated into the context and used in a variety of ways, meeting the âcomprehensiveâ descriptor for band 5.

Selecting a personal artefact, capturing real measurements, and deciding to redesign the cup show that the student has gone beyond textbook examples.  The independent choice of optimisation criteria (adding 1 cm to the rim) indicates genuine individual thinking, though the mathematical techniques themselves are standard syllabus methods.

The work is divided into an introduction, main body with clear sub-headings, and a conclusion; diagrams, figures and appendices are referenced so that supporting material does not clutter the text.  That structure is consistently maintained.  To be âexceptionallyâ organised the student would need tighter cross-referencing (for example, consistent figure numbering) and cleaner separation of modelling, calculation and reflection sections.

All key techniques (polynomial regression, definite integration, derivative optimisation) are syllabus-level and used correctly.  The student makes no use of mathematics beyond the course, but the work is fully commensurate with SL expectations, justifying band 5.

annotations.comment: The radius is rounded to 3.1âcm without mention of significantâfigure rationale. Explain your rounding choice to maintain rigour.
When rounding parameters like r and h, consider including a brief explanation of precision standards used.
Applying the truncated cone formula with base radius r and rim radius R demonstrates effective mathematical communication.
Define clearly in text that R is the rim radius (4.1âcm) before using it in equations to avoid ambiguity.
Include a clear reference to Figure 6 showing r and R on the cone sketch to strengthen presentation and reader guidance.
The surfaceâarea integral and substitution into the graphing calculator are coherently presented, showing appropriate use of technology.
The integral bounds show a typo â8â as upper limit. Ensure the correct value $$7.2524$$ is used consistently to avoid calculation errors.
Verify integration bounds against earlier defined limits before computing. This consistency check reduces propagation of errors.
Surface areas are presented numerically and rounded coherently, facilitating direct comparison of designs.
Before using the Pythagorean theorem, define a and b in the text so readers immediately understand the triangleâs sides.
Introduce the slant height calculation with a brief sentence linking to Figure 7 geometry to guide readers.

The introduction effectively situates the investigation in a personal context, demonstrating clear engagement with your familyâs traditions. This personal narrative strengthens the rationale and shows genuine independent thinking.
The aim statement is clear but repeats elements already in the rationale. Consider refining to a concise bullet list of objectives to improve coherence.
Remove or clarify the stray characters âkâ--â at the end of the introduction. Such artifacts interrupt the flow and affect presentation.
Measurements of the cupâs dimensions are clearly listed, with the height highlighted in bold. This clarity aids mathematical communication.
Cross-referencing the plotted points in Appendix 1 would benefit from consistent figure numbering (e.g., âFigure 2 in Appendix 1â) to enhance structure.
Can you explain how you chose the 22 points on the cupâs outline? Describing the selection criteria would strengthen the modelling rationale.
Reporting a correlation coefficient of 0.9908 demonstrates strong model validity and effective use of multiple representations.
When stating the horizontal bounds [0 â¤ x â¤ 7.2524], include units (cm) to reinforce clear mathematical notation.
Label the axes and units in your regression graph explicitly (e.g., âx (cm)â, ây (cm)â) to improve clarity of presentation.
The derivation of the volume integral is logically sequenced with correct $$V=\pi\int_a^b[f(x)]^2dx$$ notation, demonstrating solid understanding.
In the list, the cubic function is missing a plus sign before â0.4301x^2,â which could lead to misinterpretation. Correct the notation.
When substituting into the integral, format the expression using LaTeX for clarity (e.g., $$V=\pi\int_0^{7.2524}[f(x)]^2dx$$).
The full polynomial expansion of $$(f(x))^2$$ is lengthy. Summarize key terms and move the full expansion to an appendix to improve conciseness.
The integration steps apply the fundamental theorem correctly and are well structured, showing good understanding.
Verify the constant term in your integrated expression by differentiating your result; this reduces minor algebraic slips.
The discussion of aesthetic versus functional design demonstrates thoughtful personal engagement with the problem.
The rationale for adding 1âcm to the rim radius lacks mathematical justification. Explain why this increment optimizes aesthetics.
You mention future extensions like sustainability considerations. Reflect briefly here on environmental impact to deepen critical analysis.
The optimization of cylinder surface area via $$SA(r)=\frac{194}{r}+\pi r^2$$ and its derivative is correctly executed.
You wrote $$\frac{d(z)}{dr}$$ instead of $$\frac{d(SA)}{dr}$$; this notation slip may confuse readers. Use consistent variable names.
Include the second derivative test in equation form immediately after finding critical points to reinforce the justification for the minimum.
The second derivative test is correctly applied, confirming concavity and a valid minimum for the optimized radius.
Ensure consistent structure for URLs and access dates in works cited to enhance presentation and academic rigor.
The inequality $$200\,cm^2>89\,cm^2$$ succinctly highlights the cost-efficiency conclusion.
Include numerical cost values in this comparison section rather than referring forward, to allow readers to see results immediately.
Adding a cost-comparison table summarizing materials, surface areas and total cost would enhance presentation and reader comprehension.
Using surface areaÃthickness to model material volume is creative and demonstrates comprehensive mathematical application.
The assumption of constant wall thickness (0.2 cm) should be critically evaluated. Discuss potential variation in manufacturing.
Reflect on how tolerance in wall thickness could affect material cost estimates and propose ways to reduce this uncertainty.
Converting nickel price from ounces to USD/gram is executed accurately, showcasing correct unit handling.
After calculating nickel mass, the heading still refers to silver price. Revise the label to maintain accurate communication.
When alternating between silver and nickel sections, use consistent terminology and headings to guide the reader.
The conclusion revisits the aim and succinctly summarizes findings, providing closure to the investigation.
The conclusion omits reflection on cost-model simplifications (e.g., production costs). Adding this would deepen critical analysis.
Suggest including production cost factors and environmental impacts in future extensions to enhance reflection and real-world relevance.
The extension ideas demonstrate forward-looking critical thinking and creativity in proposing alternative investigations.
The discussion on nickel toxicity is informative but lacks quantitative environmental modelling. Consider adding a simple impact model.
Propose modelling environmental impact scores (e.g., pollutant load per cm^2) for silver vs. nickel to deepen reflection.
The works-cited list is comprehensive, reflecting a wide range of sources relevant to calculus and materials.
Citation formatting is inconsistent (missing authors, years). Adopt and apply a formal style (e.g., APA) for all entries.
